root_text.iti70:2.1: “Diṭṭhā mayā, bhikkhave, sattā kāyaduccaritena samannāgatā vacīduccaritena samannāgatā manoduccaritena samannāgatā ariyānaṁ upavādakā micchādiṭṭhikā micchādiṭṭhikammasamādānā.
root_text.iti70:2.2: Te kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā apāyaṁ duggatiṁ vinipātaṁ nirayaṁ upapannā.
root_text.iti70:3.1: Taṁ kho panāhaṁ, bhikkhave, nāññassa samaṇassa vā brāhmaṇassa vā sutvā vadāmi.
root_text.iti70:3.2: Diṭṭhā mayā, bhikkhave, sattā kāyaduccaritena samannāgatā vacīduccaritena samannāgatā manoduccaritena samannāgatā ariyānaṁ upavādakā micchādiṭṭhikā micchādiṭṭhikammasamādānā.
root_text.iti70:3.3: Te kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā apāyaṁ duggatiṁ vinipātaṁ nirayaṁ upapannā.
root_text.iti70:3.4: Api ca, bhikkhave, yadeva sāmaṁ ñātaṁ sāmaṁ diṭṭhaṁ sāmaṁ viditaṁ tadevāhaṁ vadāmi.
root_text.iti70:4.1: Diṭṭhā mayā, bhikkhave, sattā kāyaduccaritena samannāgatā vacīduccaritena samannāgatā manoduccaritena samannāgatā ariyānaṁ upavādakā micchādiṭṭhikā micchādiṭṭhikammasamādānā.
root_text.iti70:4.2: Te kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā apāyaṁ duggatiṁ vinipātaṁ nirayaṁ upapannā”ti.
root_text.iti70:7.1: Ayampi attho vutto bhagavatā, iti me sutanti.

translation_text.iti70:1.1: This was said by the Buddha, the Perfected One: that is what I heard.
translation_text.iti70:2.1: “Mendicants, I’ve seen beings who engaged in misconduct by body, speech, and mind, who denounced the noble ones, who had wrong views and acted accordingly.
translation_text.iti70:2.2: At the breaking up of the body, after death, they were reborn in a place of loss, a bad place, the underworld, hell.
translation_text.iti70:3.1: Now, I don’t say this because I’ve heard it from some other ascetic or brahmin.
translation_text.iti70:3.4: I only say it because I’ve known, seen, and realized it for myself.”
translation_text.iti70:5.3: and wrong bodily deeds have been done,
translation_text.iti70:6.2: though their life may be short,
translation_text.iti70:6.3: when their body breaks up, that witless person
translation_text.iti70:7.1: This too is a matter that was spoken by the Blessed One: that is what I heard.

variant_text.iti70:5.2: micchā vācañca bhāsiya → micchā vācaṁ abhāsiya (si, sya-all, pts-vp-pli1)
